correctness.description: Frozen violation counts. The guard fails on any NEW violation beyond these — it never re-fails on this known, pre-existing debt (tracked in #423 for typecheck).
correctness.captureMethod: eslint . -f json (summed errorCount/warningCount across all files); pnpm -r --no-bail run typecheck (counted `error TS` occurrences per package from the recursive log prefix).

speed.description: Gate wall-clock (the CI 'build' job: lint + build + typecheck + test, run sequentially).
speed.captureMethod: This PR is what first adds a `typecheck` step to ci.yml's build job (previously a false-PASS hole: typecheck was soft-gated in agent instructions only, never run in CI). No real CI run of the new job shape exists yet, so this number is estimated: measured local step timings for the OLD shape (eslint 33.3s + pnpm -r build 40.8s + vitest run 57.6s = 131.7s) were compared against the real CI 'build' job duration for commit 8a8f5018 (153s, via `gh api repos/toon-protocol/toon-client/actions/runs/30014129025/jobs`), giving a local->CI overhead ratio of 1.162x. That ratio was applied to the local NEW-shape sum (131.7s + typecheck 27.6s = 159.3s) to project 185s. Refresh with the real measured duration after the first post-merge CI run of this workflow shape; the 20% tolerance is partly there to absorb this estimate's error margin in the meantime.

performance.description: Gate resource cost, scoped to the same CI 'build' job as speed above (not the separate devbox-validate job, which is an unrelated environment-consistency check).
performance.captureMethod: runnerMinutesEstimate = gateWallClockSeconds / 60 (single ubuntu-latest runner, no parallel jobs within 'build'), carrying the same estimate caveat as speed above. imageSizeBytes and cacheHitRatePercent have no baseline yet: no Docker daemon was available in the capture environment (imageSizeBytes), and no reproducible source for pnpm/actions cache hit-rate was available outside a live CI run (cacheHitRatePercent). Both are null so the guard SKIPS those checks rather than comparing against a fabricated number (no false FAIL) — see .sandcastle/gate-guard.ts. agent-image.yml now reports image size on its own runs so imageSizeBytes can be backfilled from there.
